warning_if_exceeded: Severe hepatotoxicity and acute liver failure. Risk sharply increases above 4g/day or with concurrent alcohol use.
special_risk_groups: Hepatic impairment: reduce dose or avoid

warning_if_exceeded: GI bleeding, peptic ulcer, renal failure, increased cardiovascular events above OTC doses.
special_risk_groups: Elderly: high GI and renal risk

warning_if_exceeded: Combined hepatotoxicity (paracetamol) + GI/renal risk (ibuprofen). Do not combine with other paracetamol-containing products.
special_risk_groups: Elderly: high GI and renal risk from ibuprofen component

warning_if_exceeded: GI bleeding, Reye's syndrome in children, increased bleeding risk, tinnitus at high doses.
Excessive sedation, urinary retention, somnolence.
special_risk_groups: Renal impairment: reduce to 5 mg every other day (CrCl 11–31 mL/min)
Children 2–6 years: 2.5 mg once or twice daily

warning_if_exceeded: Lactic acidosis (rare but life-threatening), GI intolerance, vitamin B12 deficiency with chronic use.
special_risk_groups: eGFR 30–45: use with caution, reduce dose
Contrast dye procedures: hold 48 hours before and after
Heart failure requiring pharmacological treatment: contraindicated

warning_if_exceeded: Excessive hypotension, peripheral edema, reflex tachycardia.
special_risk_groups: Hepatic impairment: start at 2.5 mg; titrate slowly
Severe aortic stenosis: use with caution

warning_if_exceeded: QT interval prolongation, hepatotoxicity, CDiff colitis with prolonged use.
special_risk_groups: Hepatic impairment: avoid in severe hepatic disease
QT prolongation risk (with other QT drugs): avoid combination

max_daily_dose: 3000 mg/day (standard); up to 6000 mg/day in severe infections
warning_if_exceeded: Seizures (very high doses), antibiotic-associated diarrhea, C. difficile colitis.
special_risk_groups: Renal impairment: reduce dose if eGFR <30
Glandular fever (EBV): rash risk if prescribed inappropriately

usual_single_dose: 625 mg (standard tablet: Amoxicillin 500 mg + Clavulanate 125 mg)
max_daily_dose: 1875 mg amoxicillin + 375 mg clavulanate per day (3 tablets)
warning_if_exceeded: Cholestatic jaundice and hepatic dysfunction (clavulanate-related), GI disturbance, C. diff.
special_risk_groups: Hepatic impairment: contraindicated in history of Augmentin-related cholestasis
Renal impairment: dose reduce based on GFR

warning_if_exceeded: Hypomagnesemia (prolonged use >1 year), C. diff risk, bone fractures with long-term use, vitamin B12 deficiency.
special_risk_groups: Long-term use (>1 year): monitor B12, magnesium, bone density

warning_if_exceeded: Same class as pantoprazole: hypomagnesemia, C. diff, bone fractures, B12 deficiency with prolonged use.
special_risk_groups: Concurrent clopidogrel: omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19 — reduces antiplatelet effect

usual_single_dose: 200–400 mL per loose stool or episode of vomiting
max_daily_dose: No fixed maximum; guided by dehydration assessment
frequency: After each loose stool; sip continuously if vomiting
warning_if_exceeded: Hypernatremia in infants if given in excess without plain water. Overhydration risk in renal failure.
special_risk_groups: Renal failure: use with medical supervision
Infants under 6 months: should be given under medical guidance
Severe dehydration: IV fluids required; ORS is adjunct

max_daily_dose: Individualized; typically 2–10 mg/day; no absolute cap (INR-guided)
warning_if_exceeded: Life-threatening bleeding — intracranial hemorrhage, GI bleed, haemoptysis. INR must be monitored.
special_risk_groups: Elderly: increased bleeding risk; lower target INR
Pregnancy: absolute contraindication in first trimester and near delivery
Many drug and food interactions: requires INR monitoring

frequency: Once daily on empty stomach, 30–60 min before food
warning_if_exceeded: Thyrotoxicosis: tachycardia, arrhythmia, bone loss, adrenal crisis in undiagnosed adrenal insufficiency.
special_risk_groups: Cardiovascular disease: start very low (12.5–25 mcg), titrate slowly
Adrenal insufficiency: must treat before levothyroxine initiation
Pregnancy: TSH targets differ; require more frequent monitoring

warning_if_exceeded: Myopathy, rhabdomyolysis (especially at 80 mg), hepatotoxicity.
special_risk_groups: CYP3A4 inhibitors (e.g., clarithromycin, fluconazole): increase statin levels, myopathy risk

warning_if_exceeded: Neurotoxicity (peripheral neuropathy, encephalopathy) with prolonged use or high doses. Disulfiram-like reaction with alcohol.
special_risk_groups: Alcohol use: absolute contraindication during and 48 hours after treatment

warning_if_exceeded: QT prolongation, tendinopathy/tendon rupture (especially Achilles), peripheral neuropathy, CNS effects.
special_risk_groups: Children and adolescents: avoid (arthropathy risk); only used in specific indications
Concurrent corticosteroids: high tendon rupture risk

warning_if_exceeded: QT prolongation and serious cardiac arrhythmias (especially with CYP3A4 inhibitors). Hyperprolactinaemia with long-term use.
special_risk_groups: Hepatic impairment: contraindicated in moderate-severe disease

warning_if_exceeded: Neuropsychiatric effects (agitation, depression, suicidal thinking) — FDA black box warning (2020).
special_risk_groups: History of psychiatric disorders: prescribe with caution; monitor closely
Children: neuropsychiatric events reported — monitor behavior

warning_if_exceeded: NDMA contamination concern (reason for global withdrawal in 2020). Headache, GI upset, confusion in elderly at high doses.
special_risk_groups: WITHDRAWN from Indian and global markets by CDSCO/FDA in 2020 due to NDMA contamination
Renal impairment: reduce dose if used in approved alternative formulations

frequency: Once or twice daily on empty stomach
warning_if_exceeded: GI irritation, constipation, dark stools. Iron toxicity (acute overdose) is life-threatening, especially in children.
special_risk_groups: Children: keep out of reach — acute iron overdose is a medical emergency
First trimester pregnancy: use with caution

warning_if_exceeded: Hypercalcemia, nephrolithiasis, milk-alkali syndrome, constipation. Impairs absorption of iron, thyroid medication, bisphosphonates.

usual_single_dose: 4 mg initial, then 2 mg after each loose stool
frequency: As needed after each loose stool
warning_if_exceeded: Serious cardiac events (QT prolongation, torsades) with doses > 16 mg/day; ileus with prolonged use.
special_risk_groups: Bloody diarrhea/fever: avoid — may worsen infectious diarrhea

warning_if_exceeded: Respiratory depression, coma, dependence and withdrawal syndrome. Do NOT combine with opioids.
special_risk_groups: Elderly: increased fall and sedation risk; start at 0.25 mg
Schedule H drug in India — requires prescription
